title: New ransomware campaign targeting cloud infrastructure
description: Security researchers have identified a new ransomware variant specifically designed to target cloud-based infrastructure. The campaign uses compromised CI/CD pipelines to deploy malicious code.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The ransomware campaign targeting cloud infrastructure poses a significant threat to organizations relying on cloud services. By exploiting compromised CI/CD pipelines, attackers can deploy malicious code, potentially leading to data encryption, service disruption, and financial losses.
recommended_actions: Implement strict access controls and multi-factor authentication (MFA) for CI/CD pipelines to prevent unauthorized access.
Conduct regular security audits and vulnerability assessments of cloud infrastructure and CI/CD environments.
Establish robust backup and disaster recovery plans, ensuring critical data is stored securely and can be restored quickly in case of an attack.

title: Show HN: Open-source vulnerability scanner
description: New tool automatically scans dependencies for known vulnerabilities and suggests patches. Gaining traction in the security community.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with low severity.

title:
A vulnerability was identified in MEPIS RM, an industrial
software product developed by Metronik....
description:
A vulnerability was identified in MEPIS RM, an industrial
software product developed by Metronik. The application contained a hardcoded
cryptographic key within the Mx.Web.ComponentModel.dll component. When the
option to store domain passwords was enabled, this key was used to encrypt user
passwords before storing them in the application’s database. An attacker with
sufficient privileges to access the database could extract the encrypted
passwords, decrypt them using the embedded key, and gain u

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability allows attackers with database access to decrypt stored domain passwords due to the use of a hardcoded cryptographic key. This could lead to unauthorized access to sensitive systems and data, potentially compromising industrial operations.
recommended_actions: Immediately apply patches or updates provided by Metronik to address the vulnerability.
Disable the option to store domain passwords in the application until a secure fix is implemented.
Conduct a security audit to identify unauthorized access to the database and monitor for suspicious activity.

description: Blind server-side request forgery (SSRF) vulnerability in legacy connection methods of document co-authoring features in M-Files Server before 26.3 allow an unauthenticated attacker to cause the server to send HTTP GET requests to arbitrary URLs.

title: A vulnerability was detected in Harvard University IQSS Dataverse up to 6.8. This affects an...
description: A vulnerability was detected in Harvard University IQSS Dataverse up to 6.8. This affects an unknown function of the file /ThemeAndWidgets.xhtml of the component Theme Customization. Performing a manipulation of the argument uploadLogo results in unrestricted upload. Remote exploitation of the attack is possible. The exploit is now public and may be used. Upgrading to version 6.10 mitigates this issue. You should upgrade the affected component. The vendor was contacted early, responded in a very

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability in the Theme Customization component of Harvard University IQSS Dataverse up to version 6.8 allows for unrestricted file uploads via the 'uploadLogo' parameter. This could enable attackers to upload malicious files, potentially leading to remote code execution, data breaches, or defacement of the application.
recommended_actions: Immediately upgrade to version 6.10 or the latest available version to mitigate the vulnerability.
Conduct a thorough review of server logs to identify any signs of exploitation or unauthorized file uploads.
Implement additional security controls, such as file type validation and antivirus scanning, for all file uploads.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The SQL injection vulnerability in the articles webservice endpoint could allow attackers to manipulate database queries, potentially leading to data breaches, unauthorized data access, or database corruption.
recommended_actions: Implement parameterized queries or prepared statements to prevent SQL injection.
Conduct a thorough code review of the articles webservice endpoint to identify and fix improper order clause construction.
Perform regular security testing, including automated vulnerability scans and penetration testing, to detect similar issues.

title:
Ericsson Packet Core Controller (PCC) versions prior
to 1.38 contain a vulnerability where an...
description:
Ericsson Packet Core Controller (PCC) versions prior
to 1.38 contain a vulnerability where an attacker sending a large volume of
specially crafted messages may cause service degradation.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability in Ericsson Packet Core Controller (PCC) versions prior to 1.38 could allow attackers to send a large volume of specially crafted messages, potentially causing service degradation. This could lead to disruptions in network operations and impact the availability of critical services.
affected_industries: Enterprises relying on mobile network infrastructure
recommended_actions: Upgrade Ericsson Packet Core Controller (PCC) to version 1.38 or later to mitigate the vulnerability.
Implement rate-limiting and traffic filtering mechanisms to detect and block malicious traffic patterns.
Conduct regular vulnerability assessments and penetration testing to identify and address similar issues proactively.

title: A vulnerability was identified in Shandong Hoteam InforCenter PLM up to 8.3.8. The impacted...
description: A vulnerability was identified in Shandong Hoteam InforCenter PLM up to 8.3.8. The impacted element is the function uploadFileToIIS of the file /Base/BaseHandler.ashx. The manipulation of the argument File leads to unrestricted upload. It is possible to initiate the attack remotely. The exploit is publicly available and might be used. The vendor was contacted early about this disclosure but did not respond in any way.

title:
In the Linux kernel, the following vulnerability has been resolved:

apparmor: fix differential...
description:
In the Linux kernel, the following vulnerability has been resolved:

apparmor: fix differential encoding verification

Differential encoding allows loops to be created if it is abused. To
prevent this the unpack should verify that a diff-encode chain
terminates.

Unfortunately the differential encode verification had two bugs.

1. it conflated states that had gone through check and already been
   marked, with states that were currently being checked and marked.
   This means that loops in the c

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with unknown severity. CVE: CVE-2026-23409.

title:
In the Linux kernel, the following vulnerability has been resolved:

apparmor: fix race on...
description:
In the Linux kernel, the following vulnerability has been resolved:

apparmor: fix race on rawdata dereference

There is a race condition that leads to a use-after-free situation:
because the rawdata inodes are not refcounted, an attacker can start
open()ing one of the rawdata files, and at the same time remove the
last reference to this rawdata (by removing the corresponding profile,
for example), which frees its struct aa_loaddata; as a result, when
seq_rawdata_open() is reached, i_private is

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with unknown severity. CVE: CVE-2026-23410.

title: Improper Neutralization of Input During Web Page Generation ('Cross-site Scripting')...
description: Improper Neutralization of Input During Web Page Generation ('Cross-site Scripting') vulnerability in Brainstorm Force Ultimate Addons for WPBakery Page Builder allows DOM-Based XSS.This issue affects Ultimate Addons for WPBakery Page Builder: from n/a before 3.21.4.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability allows attackers to execute arbitrary scripts in the context of a user's browser, potentially leading to data theft, session hijacking, or malicious redirection. This could compromise sensitive user information and damage the reputation of affected websites.
affected_industries: Small and Medium Businesses (SMBs) with WordPress websites
recommended_actions: Update the Ultimate Addons for WPBakery Page Builder plugin to version 3.21.4 or later to patch the vulnerability.
Implement a web application firewall (WAF) to detect and block malicious input targeting this vulnerability.
Conduct a security audit of your WordPress site to identify and remediate any other potential vulnerabilities.

title:
In the Linux kernel, the following vulnerability has been resolved:

apparmor: fix race between...
description:
In the Linux kernel, the following vulnerability has been resolved:

apparmor: fix race between freeing data and fs accessing it

AppArmor was putting the reference to i_private data on its end after
removing the original entry from the file system. However the inode
can aand does live beyond that point and it is possible that some of
the fs call back functions will be invoked after the reference has
been put, which results in a race between freeing the data and
accessing it through the fs.

Whi

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with unknown severity. CVE: CVE-2026-23411.

title:
In the Linux kernel, the following vulnerability has been resolved:

apparmor: Fix double free of...

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability in the Linux kernel's AppArmor module may lead to a double-free error, potentially allowing attackers to exploit memory corruption, cause denial of service, or execute arbitrary code. This poses a significant risk to systems relying on AppArmor for security enforcement.
recommended_actions: Apply the latest Linux kernel patches that address CVE-2026-23408 to all affected systems.
Conduct a thorough review of systems using AppArmor to ensure no unauthorized changes or suspicious activities have occurred.
Implement robust monitoring and logging to detect potential exploitation attempts targeting this vulnerability.

title:
In the Linux kernel, the following vulnerability has been resolved:

apparmor: fix missing bounds...
description:
In the Linux kernel, the following vulnerability has been resolved:

apparmor: fix missing bounds check on DEFAULT table in verify_dfa()

The verify_dfa() function only checks DEFAULT_TABLE bounds when the state
is not differentially encoded.

When the verification loop traverses the differential encoding chain,
it reads k = DEFAULT_TABLE[j] and uses k as an array index without
validation. A malformed DFA with DEFAULT_TABLE[j] >= state_count,
therefore, causes both out-of-bounds reads and writes

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with unknown severity. CVE: CVE-2026-23407.

title:
In the Linux kernel, the following vulnerability has been resolved:

apparmor: fix side-effect...
description:
In the Linux kernel, the following vulnerability has been resolved:

apparmor: fix side-effect bug in match_char() macro usage

The match_char() macro evaluates its character parameter multiple
times when traversing differential encoding chains. When invoked
with *str++, the string pointer advances on each iteration of the
inner do-while loop, causing the DFA to check different characters
at each iteration and therefore skip input characters.
This results in out-of-bounds reads when the pointer

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability in the Linux kernel's AppArmor subsystem could lead to out-of-bounds reads, potentially exposing sensitive data or causing system instability. This issue may be exploited in certain scenarios to bypass security mechanisms or crash affected systems.
recommended_actions: Immediately update to the latest Linux kernel version that includes the patch for CVE-2026-23406.
Audit systems running AppArmor to ensure proper configuration and monitor for unusual activity.
Implement additional monitoring and intrusion detection systems to identify potential exploitation attempts.

title:
In the Linux kernel, the following vulnerability has been resolved:

apparmor: replace recursive...
description:
In the Linux kernel, the following vulnerability has been resolved:

apparmor: replace recursive profile removal with iterative approach

The profile removal code uses recursion when removing nested profiles,
which can lead to kernel stack exhaustion and system crashes.

Reproducer:
  $ pf='a'; for ((i=0; i<1024; i++)); do
      echo -e "profile $pf { \n }" | apparmor_parser -K -a;
      pf="$pf//x";
  done
  $ echo -n a > /sys/kernel/security/apparmor/.remove

Replace the recursive __aa_profile

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with unknown severity. CVE: CVE-2026-23404.

title: CVE-2026-20668 - A logging issue was addressed with improved data redaction. This issue is fixed in iOS 18.7.7 and iP
description: A logging issue was addressed with improved data redaction. This issue is fixed in iOS 18.7.7 and iPadOS 18.7.7, iOS 26.3 and iPadOS 26.3, macOS Sequoia 15.7.5, macOS Sonoma 14.8.5, macOS Tahoe 26.3, visionOS 26.3. An app may be able to access sensitive user data.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability allows an app to access sensitive user data due to insufficient data redaction in logging mechanisms. This could lead to unauthorized data exposure, privacy violations, or potential exploitation of sensitive information.
recommended_actions: Update all affected devices and operating systems to the latest patched versions (iOS 18.7.7, iPadOS 18.7.7, macOS Sequoia 15.7.5, etc.).
Review application permissions and logging configurations to ensure sensitive data is not unnecessarily exposed.
Conduct a security audit of apps running on affected devices to identify potential misuse of the vulnerability.
